en: If you could fight anyone, who would you fight?
ko: 아무나 싸울 수 있다면, 누구랑 싸울 거야?

en: Fight my boss, probably. Really?
Yeah, why? Who would you fight?
ko: 응, 왜? 너는 누구랑 싸울 건데?

en: Fight my dad.
I don't know my dad.
I mean, I know him, but he left when I was, like, six years old.
ko: 아, 알긴 아는데, 내가 여섯 살쯤에 떠났어.

en: Married this other woman and had some other kids.
ko: 다른 여자랑 결혼하고 다른 애들도 낳았지.

en: And he, like, did this every six years.
He goes to a new city and starts a new family.
ko: 새로운 도시에 가서 새 가족을 만들어.

en: Fucker setting up franchises.
My dad never went to college, so it was real important that I go.
ko: 우리 아버지는 대학을 안 가서, 내가 가는 게 진짜 중요했어.

en: That sounds familiar. So I graduate.
I call him up long distance.
I say, Dad, now what?
He says, get a job.
Same here. Now I'm 25.
I make my yearly call again.
ko: 또 일 년에 한 번 전화를 걸지.

en: I say, Dad, now what? He says, I don't know. Get married.
ko: 아빠, 이제 뭐 해? 하니까, 모르겠다, 결혼해, 라고 하더라.

en: Dad, I mean, you can't get married.
I'm a 30-year-old boy.
We're a generation of men raised by women.
I'm wondering if another woman is really the answer we need.
ko: 또 다른 여자가 정말 우리에게 필요한 답인지 모르겠어.
